_readme: Maintain the list of official data releases you want included here.
Each agency's list contains EXACT (case-sensitive) title strings as
they appear in that agency's own official calendar/ICS feed -- the
scrapers match against these verbatim, so if an agency renames a
This file only controls WHICH releases are included -- it does not
contain any dates itself. Dates are always fetched live from the
BLS: Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey
U.S. Import and Export Price Indexes

_notes.PCE: PCE (the Personal Consumption Expenditures price index) is published by BEA as part of the 'Personal Income and Outlays' release -- BEA titles vary by month (e.g. 'Personal Income and Outlays, June 2026'), so matching is prefix-based (startswith), not exact, for BEA entries.
_notes.Jobless Claims: Weekly initial jobless claims are published by the Department of Labor's ETA -- covered separately by scrapers/dol_scraper.py, not through this watchlist (DOL has only one release type, so there's nothing to select among). See MAINTENANCE.md.
_notes.ECI/JOLTS/Import-Export: Added 2026-07-01. Titles verified against a live fetch of BLS's own bls.ics feed, not guessed -- 'Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey' includes 'Survey' (BLS's website menu sometimes shortens it to just 'Job Openings and Labor Turnover' without 'Survey' -- the ICS SUMMARY field is the one that matters here and does include it). 'U.S. Import and Export Price Indexes' is plural ('Indexes') and lists Import before Export, in that exact order.
_notes.State JOLTS: Note BLS also publishes a separate 'State Job Openings and Labor Turnover' release (state-level, not the national JOLTS). It is NOT included by the 'Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey' entry above since the titles differ and matching is prefix-based -- add it separately here if you want state-level data too.
_notes.NFP / Non-Farm Payrolls: NFP is NOT a separate BLS release -- it's the headline figure inside BLS's monthly 'Employment Situation' report (released ~first Friday of each month, 8:30am ET). Added 2026-07-01, title verified against the live bls.ics feed. 'Employment Situation' also covers the unemployment rate, average hourly earnings, etc. -- everything in that single report, not just the payrolls number.
